title: Use Bambus2 scaffolding module
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2kbcg","text":"Bambus2 is a scaffolding module that can be applicable to metagenomics settings. MetaVelvet uses a novel graph splitting algorithm during contiging process, and uses the scaffolding module of Velvet (RockBand and Pebble) during scaffolding process. Alternatively, users can output MetaVelvet contigs and uses Bambus2 scaffolding module instead of using RockBand and Pebble.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"d1har","text":"For installation and getting started protocol click here. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":52,"length":4}],"data":[]},{"key":"fibdf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"lvvm","text":"Frequently Asked Questions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqdm0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b17r2","text":"Q: meta-velveth is not generated in the new version (>= 1.1.01). Is it problem? ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9r1ql","text":"A: This is not problem. The usage of MetaVelvet is changed when the version 1.1.01 is released, and the new version does not include meta-velveth. Instead, please use velveth, velvetg, and meta-velvetg.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":133,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":167,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":176,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":189,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"du85m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ed4g2","text":"Q: When only one coverage peak is detected (or manually input), is there any difference between MetaVelvet and Velvet algorithms? 
A: There is no substantial difference. In such cases, meta-velvetg moves to "single-peak mode" and graph splitting functions in meta-velvetg is not called. Instead of graph splitting functions, standard velvet functions are called in such cases.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":131,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":185,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":259,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"94c6j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ba1v1","text":"Q: What's the difference in working procedures between velvetg, meta-velvetg (<= 0.3.1), and meta-velvetg(>= 1.1.01)? 
A: The following is the working procedure of velvetg, meta-velvetg (<= 0.3.1), and meta-velvetg (>= 1.1.01): 

 velvetg & meta-velvetg(<=0.3.1) : 
        Load Sequences & Roadmaps file 
        -> Generate PreGraph file 
        -> Generate Graph or Graph2 file 
        -> Generate contigs.fa and LastGraph 

 meta-velvetg (>= 1.1.01): 
        Load Sequences & Roadmaps & Graph2 file 
        -> Generate meta-velvetg.contigs.fa and meta-velvetg.LastGraph ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":55,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":64,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":93,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":119,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":164,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":173,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":202,"length":12},{"style":"bold","offset":231,"length":34},{"style":"bold","offset":431,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"77suc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"84tq0","text":"Q: Is version compatibility between Velvet and MetaVelvet fully tested? 
A: Version compatibility between Velvet-1.0.06 and MetaVelvet-1.1.01 is fully tested.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":73,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"41eij","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bvpge","text":"Troubleshooting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"76r9a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"82g52","text":"Trouble:When drawingk-mer coverage histogram (as in the"Advanced topics 1"section), the following warning messages is appeared:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":20,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":55,"length":19}],"data":[]},{"key":"ed1vg","text":"> weighted.hist(data$shot1_cov,data$lgth,breaks=seq(0,200,1)) 
Warning messages: 
1: In min(x, na.rm = na.rm) : 
no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf 
2: In max(x, na.rm = na.rm) : 
no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf 
3: In weighted.hist(data$shot1_cov, data$lgth, breaks = seq(0, 200, : 
Areas will not relate to frequencies 

","type":"blockquote","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c396s","text":"Solution:This warning (error) is caused by "Inf" values in the Graph2 node stats. Accordingly, by removing "Inf" values from the Graph2 stats, the error is resolved:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"23mtu","text":"$ head -n 1 meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.txt \ 
        > meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.rmInf.txt 
$ perl -ne '{print $_ unless /Inf/;}' \ 
        meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.txt \ 
        >> meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.rmInf.txt 
$ R 
> library(plotrix) 
> data = read.table("meta-velvetg.Graph2-stats.rmInf.txt", header=TRUE) 
> weighted.hist(data$shot1_cov,data$lgth,breaks=seq(0,200,1)) ","type":"blockquote","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"115t","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"87D9109CA319486A82015EB63AFDB5E5","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/MetaVelvet-Installation-and-Getting-Started-d5k84v"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"99E67C0F53214B5480400DB0E8DA8F07","url":"http://metavelvet.dna.bio.keio.ac.jp/MV.html#advanced-topic-1"}}}}
steps: To install Bambus2 programs, please see their web site: http://www.cbcb.umd.edu/software/bambus/
Execute velveth , velvetg as in the " Getting started " protocol. Please check that " out-dir /Sequences", " out-dir /Roadmaps", and " out-dir /Graph2" files are created.

Execute meta-velvetg with -amos_file and -scaffolding options. Please check that " out-dir /meta-velvetg.asm.afg" file is created. The file is meta-velvetg contiging result in an AMOS format. ~$ meta-velvetg out-dir -ins_length 260 \ -exp_covs 214_122_70_43_25_13.5 \ -amos_file yes -scaffolding no
Create amos bank. ~$ bank-transact -m out-dir/meta-velvetg.asm.afg -b bank-dir -cf
Use the paired-end information to construct a collection of contig links.
Bundle the contig links into a collection of contig edges.
Identify genomic repeats and output them.
Order and orient contigs according to repeat and link information.
Output contig FastA file. ~$ bank2fasta -d -b bank-dir \ | tee contig-file
Then, scaffold-file is the final scaffolding results in the FastA format.
creator: Bonnie L Hurwitz
